text:
This article is about the Evangelion from the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series. For the Evangelion from Rebuild of Evangelion film series, see Evangelion Unit-02 (Rebuild).

 is the third Evangelion completed, and the first Production Model Evangelion. The design of Unit-02 supposedly rectifies the mistakes made during the construction of Prototype Unit-00 and Test Type Unit-01, making it the first Evangelion built specifically for combat against the Angels. It is piloted by Asuka Langley Sohryu and briefly by Kaworu Nagisa.

Profile

Unit-02 is a Production Model Evangelion and uses the same armor arrangement as the succeeding Unit-03 and Unit-04, along with the upgraded Unit-00'. All Evangelions using this arrangement have the same shoulder pylon design (with the exception of the Mass Production Evangelions, which don't have shoulder pylons, and Evangelion Unit-01, which has a slightly different shoulder pylon design) and five V-shaped armor plates on their torso.

Evangelion Unit-02 is unique in having bluish-purple blood, blue-gray skin, and four fiery orange eyes, as all other Evangelions (including Unit-00, and aside from the Mass Production units) possess red blood, two eyes, and brown skin.

Although Unit-02 has a jaw and mouth, when armored, these cannot open to the same extent as those on Unit-01, Unit-03 and Unit-04 due to the lack of a hinged jaw piece in the helmet. Instead, the helmet has movable sections that can shift to cover or uncover the Evangelion's eyes. Four prominent nostril holes are located in the bulging forehead section of the helmet.

An altered Unit-02 design was adopted for The End of Evangelion. Although visually similar to the original, the new design has more human-like body proportions, drastically reduced pylon size, and a narrower, redesigned head shape. The blades of armor on the lower arms have also been eliminated. Some of these changes may have been made in order to ease the animation of Unit-02's final battle, although they do add a slight visual discontinuity with the rest of the series. The "helmet-popping" mechanism is also eliminated in the movie version. Although Unit-02 is never seen opening its jaws while the head is fully covered (despite the fact that it roars and makes other vocalizations), the jaw piece, when exposed later, visibly has "teeth" as per Unit-01 and others.

Features

As a Production Model Evangelion, Unit-02 is shown to be capable of using different equipment, like the D-type Equipment seen in Episode 10. In addition, Unit-02 has a distinctive Progressive Knife, the PK-2, that resembles a box cutter. Seemingly unique to Unit-02 is the , a rack of 7 spikes in its right shoulder pylon which can be fired at close combat, piercing its target.

Unit-02's soul is that of Kyoko Zeppelin Sohryu. For this reason her daughter, Asuka Langley Sohryu was chosen as her pilot. However, only the portion of Kyoko's soul containing her maternal aspects was absorbed into the Eva. As a result, while Kyoko physically survived the contact experiment with Unit-02, she was driven clinically insane and had to be institutionalized, profoundly affecting her young daughter Asuka.

Evidence suggests that the remainder of Kyoko's soul was salvaged into Unit-02 following Kyoko's suicide. During the scene in Episode 25' where Asuka learns her mother is inside Unit-02, Kyoko repeats, "You mustn't die! You must live! You're alive!", several times. However, the familiar "Please die with me!" (as heard in the Episode 22 and Episode 25 flashback sequences) is mixed in twice among her inspirational words, suggesting that the insane Kyoko and the maternal Kyoko were incorporated back together.

History

Neon Genesis Evangelion

Unit-02 was first introduced in Episode 08 of the series, and was activated to fight Gaghiel despite having no combat gear aside from its Progressive Knife (the Needle Launcher had not been introduced yet). The Evangelion was severely hampered in underwater combat but succeeded in prying open the Angel's mouth and neutralizing its A.T. Field, allowing two scuttled Iowa-class battleships to explode inside and destroy its core.

The Evangelion took part in several other battles, acting in concert with Unit-01 to defeat Israfel, and then being sent into a volcano in the specialized D-Type diving equipment to capture the Angel Sandalphon. The capture of Sandalphon failed, resulting in a battle with the Angel. Unit-02 lost a leg due to an exploding blasting bolt, and had to be recovered from the magma by Unit-01. Unit-02 also took part in the Sahaquiel run, and dealt the fatal blow by stabbing the Angel in the core with its Progressive Knife.

The Unit-02's successful combat record in the TV series virtually ended at this point. The Bardiel battle saw Unit-02 being incapacitated early on when Asuka was distracted and surprised, having done no damage to the infected Unit-03. The battle with Zeruel was even more disastrous, with all of Unit-02's gun and rocket fire proving utterly ineffectual and its head and arms being cut off by the Angel. Unit-02 was repaired and sent into battle against the Angel Arael, where both pilot and resident soul inside suffered from the Angel's mental probing. The sync rate between Asuka and Unit-02 was so low that the Evangelion was incapable of motion in the battle against Armisael and had to be withdrawn from combat. Finally, in Episode 24, Kaworu Nagisa controlled Unit-02 externally and used it to break through barriers on the way to Terminal Dogma and had it fight Unit-01, where it was pierced in the head and damaged.

The End of Evangelion

Unit-02's final combat operation occurred in The End of Evangelion, when Asuka was sent in Unit-02 to the bottom of the GeoFront lake, where it was subject to depth charge attacks from JSSDF forces. Eventually, Asuka came into contact with the soul of her mother, Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu, which was, unbeknownst to her, inside Unit-02 all along. The Evangelion entered an elevated power state somewhat similar to berserk mode, with Asuka seeming to achieve an unusually high synch ratio. The Eva easily destroyed JSSDF tanks, VTOLs, and the frigate in the lake, but had its umbilical cable severed during the fight and was forced to switch to battery power.

In the subsequent battle against the Mass Production Evangelions, Unit-02 severely damaged all nine of them in a little over three minutes, only to be struck down by a replica Spear of Longinus just as its battery ran out of power. The Mass Production Evangelions reactivated and proceeded to mutilate and disembowel Unit-02. Afterwards, Unit-02 went into a strange variation of the unpowered berserk state for the first and final time before being struck by eight more Spears, the last of which struck its core. The dismembered corpse of the Eva was later seen being carried by the Mass Production Evangelions, and was ultimately dropped onto the floor of the Geofront, where it stared with dead eyes up at the events unfolding in the sky.

Other Media

Neon Genesis Evangelion manga

In the manga, Unit-02 plays a similar role as its anime counterpart at first, however its role in the story and fate greatly diverge later in the story.

Unit-02 is piloted by Kaworu Nagisa in the battle against Armisael, during which it wields the Dual Saw (a weapon that debuted in the Neon Genesis Evangelions video-game).

As in the anime, Asuka pilots Unit-02 against the Mass Production Evangelion. Both Asuka and Unit-02 are spared from complete dismemberment, only losing its right arm. They are saved by Unit-01 wielding one of the Mass Production Evangelion's swords. Shinji claims that he is there due to a promise by Misato to save Asuka. He defeats the Mass Production Evangelions but those activate later and hold Unit-01 down. Unit-02 is last seen engulfed in an explosion as Unit-01 and the Mass Production Evangelion rise in the air to begin Third Impact. Neon Genesis Evangelion Volume 13 

Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA

Evangelion Unit-02 itself only appears during the battle against the Mass Production Evangelions where it struggles against them before Shinji and Rei arrive in their Evangelion Unit-01 Type F and Evangelion Unit-00 Type F respectively to destroy them. The Evangelions piloted by Asuka and Rei change their overall proportions mirroring Asuka and Rei's own bodies as they mature as women over 3 years. Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA Volume 1, Chapter 02 

At a certain point, Unit-02’s restraint armor no longer fit despite the best efforts of the mechanics. The armor was removed, and new, properly fitting, Stage 2 restraint armor was created. Due to the results of performance in Unit-02, the same kind of armor was used on the Evangelion Unit-0.0. Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA Volume 1, Chapter 02 The Unit-02 will later be upgraded into its Stage 2 form, the Evangelion Unit-02 Type II.

More about the development of the original Unit-02 is also revealed in Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA. A testbed body was first used to test the Unit-02 specification before switching to its current body. This testbed body was used in the development of the Evangelion EUROII Heurtebize and apparently contains an imprint of Asuka's mother's soul.

Developement

]]
The Unit-02 seen in the Neon Genesis Evangelion Proposal is labelled "Test Type" and has two hornlike cheek extensions, along with many other design differences observable at this point. The red color scheme is already present.

In 2019, Evangelion Unit-02 was redesigned by Ikuto Yamashita in part of the METAL BUILD figure line. https://tamashii.jp/special/metalbuild/evangelion/02/
